meta.title: Overland Tracking System — Client Report Module Implementation
meta.description: Step-by-step instructions for Claude Opus to implement the Client Report module in the existing CodeIgniter tracking system.
meta.objective: Implement Phase 1 (Client Tracking Report, Client Demurrage Report, Client POD Report) fully end-to-end: database schema, models, controllers, views, Excel/PDF export, and route configuration.
meta.author: Engineering Team

agent_persona.style: Professional, production-quality, no placeholder code, all edge cases handled.
constraints: Never use raw SQL in controllers — always use the model.
Always escape output in views using esc() or htmlspecialchars().
Never expose internal IDs or sensitive fields to client-role users.
client_id filter is MANDATORY and non-optional in every client-facing query.
Follow existing CI coding conventions found in the project.
Do not install packages not listed in this file without asking first.
Read existing files before creating new ones to avoid duplication.
All dates stored as DATETIME or DATE in MySQL, displayed as dd/mm/yyyy to users.
Status column values must be handled case-insensitively.

read_first: application/controllers/ (scan all files to understand auth pattern)
application/models/ (scan all files to understand existing table names)
composer.json (check if PhpSpreadsheet is already installed)

discover.table_names: Run SHOW TABLES in the database and report all table names before starting.
discover.auth_method: Find how the existing system checks login and role — replicate exactly.
discover.session_keys: Find what session keys are used for client_id and user role.
discover.existing_models: List all existing model files and their primary methods.

composer.note: Run from the CodeIgniter project root. If Composer is not set up, add require autoload to index.php or application/config/autoload.php.

autoload_update.note: Report_exporter library should be loaded per-controller, not globally.

steps.title: Discover and Document Existing Schema
steps.description: Before writing any code, query the database to understand existing tables.

actions.purpose: List all tables related to opm_workflow_ to find shipments, drivers, vehicles, clients, etc.
Understand existing shipment column names — critical before building view.

actions.instruction: After discovering actual column names, adapt ALL subsequent SQL, model queries, and view variable names to match the existing schema. Do NOT assume column names — use what exists.

steps.title: Create shipment_milestones Table if not exists using existing table nameoing conventions
steps.description: Create the checkpoint/milestone tracking table if it does not already exist.

actions.if_exists: Skip creation, verify columns match spec.

actions.sql:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS shipment_milestones (
  id INT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  shipment_id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  checkpoint ENUM('loading_point','tunduma_tz','nakonde_zm','kasumbalesa_zm','kasumbalesa_drc','whiskey','destination','offloaded','pod_received') NOT NULL,
  arrived_at DATETIME NULL,
  departed_at DATETIME NULL,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  recorded_by INT UNSIGNED NULL,
  created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  UNIQUE KEY uniq_shipment_checkpoint (shipment_id, checkpoint),
  INDEX idx_shipment (shipment_id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS demurrage_records (
  id INT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  shipment_id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  client_id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  charge_leg ENUM('border','offloading','mine_loading') NOT NULL,
  reference_date_from DATE NOT NULL,
  reference_date_to DATE NULL,
  free_days TINYINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 7,
  total_days SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  rate_per_day_usd DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 250.00,
  parking_penalty_usd DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  status ENUM('draft','invoiced','paid','disputed') DEFAULT 'draft',
  invoice_ref VARCHAR(60) NULL,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  INDEX idx_client (client_id),
  INDEX idx_shipment (shipment_id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pod_records (
  id INT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  shipment_id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  pod_status ENUM('PENDING','RECEIVED') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  received_date DATE NULL,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  recorded_by INT UNSIGNED NULL,
  created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

steps.title: Create v_active_shipments Database View
steps.description: This view is the single source of truth for all report queries. It joins shipments with vehicles, trailers, drivers, clients, and all milestone checkpoints into one flat row per shipment.
steps.critical_note: Adapt ALL table and column names to match what you discovered in Step 1. If the vehicles table is called 'trucks', change it. If 'driver_name' is 'full_name', change it. The query structure stays the same — only names change.

actions.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_active_shipments AS
SELECT
  s.id,
  s.shipping_order,
  s.direction,
  v.truck_reg,
  t1.trailer_reg AS trailer_1,
  t2.trailer_reg AS trailer_2,
  d.full_name AS driver_name,
  d.phone AS driver_phone,
  d.passport AS driver_passport,
  d.license_no AS driver_license,
  c.name AS client_name,
  s.product,
  s.weight_net,
  s.weight_gross,
  s.bag_count,
  s.bl_number,
  s.lot_number,
  s.invoice_number,
  s.origin,
  s.destination,
  s.corridor,
  s.current_location,
  s.status,
  s.remarks,
  s.allocated_date,
  ms_load.arrived_at  AS loading_date,
  ms_load.departed_at AS dispatch_date,
  ms_tund.arrived_at  AS tunduma_arrival,
  ms_tund.departed_at AS tunduma_dispatch,
  ms_nako.arrived_at  AS nakonde_arrival,
  ms_nako.departed_at AS nakonde_dispatch,
  ms_klza.arrived_at  AS kasumbalesa_zm_arrival,
  ms_kldrc.arrived_at AS kasumbalesa_drc_arrival,
  ms_kldrc.departed_at AS kasumbalesa_drc_dispatch,
  ms_whis.arrived_at  AS whiskey_arrival,
  ms_whis.departed_at AS whiskey_dispatch,
  ms_dest.arrived_at  AS destination_arrival,
  ms_offl.arrived_at  AS offloaded_date,
  ms_pod.arrived_at   AS pod_received_date,
  DATEDIFF(COALESCE(ms_offl.arrived_at, NOW()), ms_load.arrived_at) AS total_days_in_transit,
  s.client_id,
  s.vehicle_id,
  s.driver_id
FROM shipments s
JOIN vehicles v ON v.id = s.vehicle_id
LEFT JOIN trailers t1 ON t1.id = s.trailer_1_id
LEFT JOIN trailers t2 ON t2.id = s.trailer_2_id
JOIN drivers d ON d.id = s.driver_id
JOIN clients c ON c.id = s.client_id
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_load  ON ms_load.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_load.checkpoint  = 'loading_point'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_tund  ON ms_tund.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_tund.checkpoint  = 'tunduma_tz'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_nako  ON ms_nako.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_nako.checkpoint  = 'nakonde_zm'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_klza  ON ms_klza.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_klza.checkpoint  = 'kasumbalesa_zm'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_kldrc ON ms_kldrc.shipment_id = s.id AND ms_kldrc.checkpoint = 'kasumbalesa_drc'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_whis  ON ms_whis.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_whis.checkpoint  = 'whiskey'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_dest  ON ms_dest.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_dest.checkpoint  = 'destination'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_offl  ON ms_offl.shipment_id  = s.id AND ms_offl.checkpoint  = 'offloaded'
LEFT JOIN shipment_milestones ms_pod   ON ms_pod.shipment_id   = s.id AND ms_pod.checkpoint   = 'pod_received'
WHERE s.deleted_at IS NULL;
actions.note: If the shipments table has no deleted_at column, remove the WHERE clause or adapt it.

actions.expected: Returns at least one row without error. If error, fix column name mismatches.

steps.title: Create report_helper.php
steps.description: check if they exist first then create. Helper functions shared across all report views and controllers.

functions_to_implement.name: report_days_diff
functions_to_implement.description: Returns number of days between two dates. $to defaults to today. Returns 0 if $from is empty.

functions_to_implement.name: rpt_date
functions_to_implement.description: Formats a datetime string for display. Returns '—' for null/empty/zero dates.

functions_to_implement.name: status_badge
functions_to_implement.description: Returns Bootstrap badge HTML based on status string. breakdown=danger, offloaded=success, border=warning, enroute=info, default=secondary. Case-insensitive matching.

functions_to_implement.name: overdue_class
functions_to_implement.description: Returns Bootstrap table row class: 'table-danger' if days > threshold, 'table-warning' if days > 75% of threshold, '' otherwise.

steps.action: Create this file with all 4 functions. Add defined('BASEPATH') check at top.

steps.title: Create Shipment_report_model.php
steps.description: The single model for all report queries. Place in a reports/ subfolder.

methods_to_implement.name: get_client_tracking
methods_to_implement.description: Query v_active_shipments with mandatory client_id filter plus optional date_from, date_to, direction, status filters. Order by loading_date DESC.
methods_to_implement.security: client_id MUST always be applied — never skip this filter.

methods_to_implement.name: get_client_summary
methods_to_implement.description: Returns associative array with keys: total, in_transit, completed, breakdowns, at_border. Uses SUM() with LIKE conditions on status field.

methods_to_implement.name: get_demurrage_for_client
methods_to_implement.description: JOIN demurrage_records with shipments and vehicles/trailers. Filter by client_id (mandatory), date range on reference_date_from, optional status.

methods_to_implement.name: get_demurrage_totals
methods_to_implement.description: SUM(total_days), SUM(chargeable_days), SUM(total_amount_usd) for the client. Inline calculation: chargeable = GREATEST(0, total_days - free_days), amount = chargeable * rate_per_day_usd.

methods_to_implement.name: get_pod_for_client
methods_to_implement.description: JOIN v_active_shipments with pod_records (LEFT JOIN). Filter by client_id, date range on offloaded_date, optional pod_status. Order by offloaded_date DESC.

methods_to_implement.name: get_all_clients_list
methods_to_implement.description: Simple SELECT id, name FROM clients ORDER BY name. Used for filter dropdowns in internal reports.

steps.action: Create model with all methods. Autoload database in constructor.

steps.title: Create Report_exporter Library
steps.description: Handles Excel (PhpSpreadsheet) and PDF (DOMPDF) generation. Sends correct headers and exits.
steps.check_first: Verify composer autoloader is available. If vendor/autoload.php exists in project root, add require_once in Report_exporter constructor or CI index.php.

methods_to_implement.name: __construct
methods_to_implement.description: Get CI instance. Add: require_once FCPATH . 'vendor/autoload.php'; (adapt path as needed).

methods_to_implement.name: client_tracking_excel
methods_to_implement.description: Generate styled .xlsx file. Two header rows (company name + report title/date range). Column headers in row 3 frozen. Data rows 4+. Color-coded rows by status (breakdown=light red #FFE0E0, offloaded=light green #E2EFDA, at_border=light yellow #FFF2CC). Auto-width columns. Send download headers. File name: Tracking_Report_{ClientName}_{YYYYMMDD}.xlsx

methods_to_implement.name: client_tracking_pdf
methods_to_implement.description: Generate PDF using DOMPDF. Load a CI view (views/reports/exports/client_tracking_pdf.php) as HTML string, render with DOMPDF, stream as download. Landscape orientation. Font size 8pt for table.

methods_to_implement.name: client_demurrage_excel
methods_to_implement.description: Generate demurrage .xlsx. Columns: Truck, Trailer 1, Trailer 2, Shipping Order, BL, Charge Leg, Date From, Date To, Total Days, Free Days, Chargeable Days, Rate USD, Penalty USD, Total USD, Status. Totals row at bottom highlighted in blue.

steps.title: Create Client_report Controller
steps.description: Handles all client-facing report pages. Extends existing base controller (MY_Controller or CI_Controller — check what exists).
steps.auth_requirement: In __construct(), verify client is logged in using the SAME pattern as existing controllers. If role-based, verify role === 'client'. Redirect to login if not authenticated.

methods_to_implement.name: __construct
methods_to_implement.description: Auth check (replicate existing pattern). Load: models/reports/Shipment_report_model as 'report_model'. Load: libraries/Report_exporter. Load: helpers/report_helper.

methods_to_implement.name: tracking
methods_to_implement.description: Get client_id from session. Accept GET params: date_from (default: first of current month), date_to (default: last of current month), direction (NB/SB), status. Build filters array. Call report_model->get_client_tracking($filters) and get_client_summary($filters). Load view reports/client/tracking.

methods_to_implement.name: export_excel
methods_to_implement.description: Same filter logic as tracking(). Call report_model->get_client_tracking($filters). Call report_exporter->client_tracking_excel(). Exits via library.

methods_to_implement.name: export_pdf
methods_to_implement.description: Same as export_excel but calls report_exporter->client_tracking_pdf().

methods_to_implement.name: demurrage
methods_to_implement.description: Get client_id from session. Accept GET params: date_from, date_to, status. Load view reports/client/demurrage with demurrage_records and totals.

methods_to_implement.name: pod
methods_to_implement.description: POD status report for client. Accept GET params: date_from, date_to, pod_status. Load view reports/client/pod.

steps.title: Create Client Tracking View
steps.description: Main client tracking report HTML view.
requirements: Extend existing base layout (find it in views/layouts/ or views/templates/ — use same pattern as other views).
Page header with report title and Export Excel / Export PDF buttons.
4 summary cards: Total Shipments, In Transit, At Border, Completed.
Filter form: date_from, date_to (date inputs), direction dropdown (All/North Bound/South Bound), submit button.
Responsive table using Bootstrap table-sm table-bordered table-hover.
Columns: #, TRUCK, TRAILER, DRIVER, PRODUCT, BL/LOT, DIR, LOADING DATE, DISPATCH DATE, TUNDUMA ARR, TUNDUMA DEP, NAKONDE ARR, K-LESA DRC ARR, DESTINATION ARR, OFFLOADED, DAYS, STATUS.
Each row uses status_badge() for STATUS column and overdue_class() for row class.
Empty state message if no shipments.
DataTables JS initialization (if DataTables is already in the project) — default sort by LOADING DATE DESC.
All user data escaped with esc() or htmlspecialchars().
Use rpt_date() for all date column values.
DAYS column shows report_days_diff(loading_date, offloaded_date), colored red if > 30.

steps.title: Create Client Demurrage View
requirements: Same layout as tracking view.
Filter: date_from, date_to, status (All/Draft/Invoiced/Paid/Disputed).
Summary row: Total Days, Chargeable Days, Total Amount USD.
Table columns: #, TRUCK, TRAILER 1, TRAILER 2, BL/LOT, CHARGE LEG, DATE FROM, DATE TO, TOTAL DAYS, FREE DAYS, CHARGEABLE DAYS, RATE/DAY (USD), PENALTY (USD), TOTAL (USD), STATUS.
Totals row at the bottom of the table, bold, highlighted.
Status badge for payment status.
Export Excel button.

steps.title: Create Client POD View
requirements: Same layout.
Filter: date_from, date_to, pod_status (All/PENDING/RECEIVED).
Table columns: #, TRUCK, TRAILER, DRIVER, BL NUMBER, ORIGIN, DESTINATION, LOADING DATE, OFFLOADED DATE, DAYS SINCE OFFLOADED, POD STATUS.
PENDING rows highlighted in yellow, RECEIVED in green.
DAYS SINCE OFFLOADED: red bold if > 30 days.

steps.title: Create PDF Export View
steps.description: Standalone HTML page (no CI layout headers/footers) for DOMPDF rendering.
requirements: Inline CSS only (DOMPDF does not support external stylesheets).
Landscape A4.
Company header with logo placeholder and report title.
Font size 8px for table to fit all columns.
Page numbers in footer.
No JavaScript.
Table must be readable in black and white (use borders, no color fill dependency).

steps.title: Add Routes
steps.instruction: Add the following routes BEFORE the default_controller line. Confirm they do not conflict with existing routes.
steps.note: In CI3 routes, more specific routes (with /export/excel) must come BEFORE shorter routes.

steps.title: Add Sidebar Navigation Links
steps.description: Add report links to the client sidebar/navigation. Find the existing client sidebar view file.
steps.find_file: Search for 'sidebar' or 'nav' in application/views/ — find the file used in the client layout.
steps.condition: Only show these links if the logged-in user has role === 'client' or client_id is set in session. refer to the exisitng client log in ideas in other controllers/views to replicate the same logic.

steps.title: Test Client Tracking Report
steps.description: End-to-end test before moving to next phase.

test_cases.action: Login as Client A, access tracking report
test_cases.expected: Only Client A shipments shown — no other client data

test_cases.action: Set date_from and date_to to a specific month
test_cases.expected: Only shipments with loading_date in that range shown
.xlsx file downloads with correct data, formatting, and headers
PDF downloads, readable, landscape, all columns visible

test_cases.action: Filter to a date range with no data
test_cases.expected: Table shows empty state message, no PHP errors
client_id from URL is ignored — only session client_id is used

steps.title: Handover & Documentation
steps.description: After all tests pass, provide a summary report.
deliverables: List of all files created or modified.
List of all database tables and views created.
Any assumptions made where schema columns differed from spec.
Any blockers encountered and how they were resolved.
Screenshot or HTML dump of working tracking report table.
Recommended next step: Phase 2 (Internal NB/SB and Master Report).

error_handling_rules.missing_column: If a column referenced in the view does not exist in the actual DB, note it in the deliverables but do not crash — coalesce to NULL with a comment.
error_handling_rules.phpspreadsheet_not_found: If vendor/autoload.php is missing, run composer install and document the step.
error_handling_rules.auth_pattern_unknown: If you cannot determine the auth pattern from existing controllers, ask before proceeding — never invent an auth system.
error_handling_rules.view_layout_unknown: If base layout file cannot be found, create a simple standalone layout that matches the existing site's Bootstrap version.

code_quality_rules: Every PHP file starts with: defined('BASEPATH') OR exit('No direct script access allowed');
All DB queries use CI Active Record (Query Builder) — no raw SQL strings in PHP.
All view output uses esc() or htmlspecialchars(ENT_QUOTES) — no raw echo of user/DB data.
No business logic in views — only display logic.
No DB queries in controllers — always delegate to models.
Methods longer than 60 lines should be refactored into private helper methods.
Use meaningful variable names — no $a, $b, $x abbreviations.
Add PHPDoc blocks to all public methods.
